user_problem: Users get output that looks finished but loses tables, headings, lists, code blocks, or document hierarchy.
proposed_response: Add structure-aware quality checks, degradation warnings, and clearer output fidelity signals for documents and page extraction.
success_metric: Lower silent-quality failures and better output trust.
main_risk: Complex quality heuristics may create false warnings.

user_problem: Users get a quick first win but lose trust on the first real edge case.
proposed_response: Improve first-run guidance, explain edge-case outcomes better, and add clearer next-step feedback after extraction.
success_metric: Lower abandonment after first non-trivial extraction.
main_risk: May improve messaging without fixing root causes if done superficially.

user_problem: Users cannot tell whether an incomplete result came from a static read, page complexity, or a harder rendering need.
proposed_response: Expose extraction path and dynamic-page likelihood signals so users know what Oleriq actually saw.
main_risk: Could surface limits more visibly before a fallback exists.

user_problem: Users lose trust when jobs look successful but return empty or partial output.
proposed_response: Separate processed, usable, partial, and failed states with explicit reasons.
success_metric: Fewer false-success moments and fewer empty-output surprises.
main_risk: Requires careful state design across both single and batch flows.

user_problem: Users need better reasons, retry guidance, and per-item visibility without a full agent replay stack.
proposed_response: Add per-item diagnostics, retry hints, and stronger failure reporting in batch and export flows.
success_metric: Lower support burden and faster self-serve recovery.
main_risk: Too much detail can overwhelm non-technical users if not staged well.

user_problem: Buyers distrust products with surprise consumption or unclear cost shape.
proposed_response: Keep limits explicit and present usage economics in a simple, predictable way.
success_metric: Higher willingness to try recurring workflows.
main_risk: Packaging work can distract from product-truth problems if over-prioritized.

user_problem: Teams want more confidence in filenames, reruns, and queue truth as batch usage grows.
proposed_response: Improve deterministic output naming, per-item job clarity, and rerun behavior.
main_risk: Adds operational surface area to maintain.

user_problem: Users want more than markdown when trust is already established.
proposed_response: Offer targeted structured extraction options only after fidelity and truth surfaces improve.
success_metric: Higher-value workflows without full crawler-platform sprawl.
main_risk: Users may expect more coverage than Oleriq can honestly support.

user_problem: Some users will want Oleriq inside agent workflows, but that is not the current core promise.
proposed_response: Defer MCP and deeper tool-facing packaging until the core trust layer is stronger.
main_risk: Premature agent expansion can bloat scope and dilute the product.

user_problem: Demand exists for harder pages, but the cost and complexity are high.
proposed_response: Treat anti-bot handling as a later escalation path or partnership problem rather than a near-term default promise.
main_risk: Competitors may market breadth more aggressively in the meantime.
